dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3334 IS: Laboratory Analysts and Biometric Personnel Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mrs. Shaheen (for herself and Mr. Crapo) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To prohibit reductions in the workforce at the Drug Enforcement Administration, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Laboratory Analysts and Biometric Personnel Act of 2025 or the LAB Personnel Act of 2025.
In this Act, the term laboratory workforce at the Drug Enforcement Administration means—

paragraph.text: positions in the Drug Enforcement Administration at forensic laboratories of the Drug Enforcement Administration, including—

subparagraph.text: any other positions in the Drug Enforcement Administration at such forensic laboratories; and

paragraph.text: other positions in the Drug Enforcement Administration at locations other than such a forensic laboratory that will be relocated to such a forensic laboratory that, as of the date of the enactment of this Act, is being constructed or otherwise finalized.

section.header: Prohibition on reducing the laboratory workforce at the Drug Enforcement Agency

subsection.text: The laboratory workforce at the Drug Enforcement Administration shall be exempt from any hiring freezes or workforce reductions related to spending cuts, reprogramming of funds, or the probationary status of employees.

Nothing in this section may be construed to restrict the authority of the Attorney General to manage the workforce of the Department of Justice under existing procedures in cases of misconduct or poor performance.
